originalText: שִׁיר הַשִּׁירִים אֲשֶׁר לִשְׁלֹמֹה׃
englishText: THE song of songs, which is Solomon’s.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יִשָּׁקֵנִי מִנְּשִׁיקוֹת פִּיהוּ כִּי־טוֹבִים דֹּדֶיךָ מִיָּיִן׃
englishText: Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth— For thy love is better than wine.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לְרֵיחַ שְׁמָנֶיךָ טוֹבִים שֶׁמֶן תּוּרַק שְׁמֶךָ עַל־כֵּן עֲלָמוֹת אֲהֵבוּךָ׃
englishText: Thine ointments have a goodly fragrance; Thy name is as ointment poured forth; Therefore do the maidens love thee.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מָשְׁכֵנִי אַחֲרֶיךָ נָּרוּצָה הֱבִיאַנִי הַמֶּלֶךְ חֲדָרָיו נָגִילָה וְנִשְׂמְחָה בָּךְ נַזְכִּירָה דֹדֶיךָ מִיַּיִן מֵישָׁרִים אֲהֵבוּךָ׃
englishText: Draw me, we will run after thee; The king hath brought me into his chambers; We will be glad and rejoice in thee, We will find thy love more fragrant than wine! Sincerely do they love thee.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שְׁחוֹרָה אֲנִי וְנָאוָה בְּנוֹת יְרוּשָׁלִָם כְּאָהֳלֵי קֵדָר כִּירִיעוֹת שְׁלֹמֹה׃
englishText: ’I am black, but comely, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, As the tents of Kedar, As the curtains of Solomon.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אַל־תִּרְאוּנִי שֶׁאֲנִי שְׁחַרְחֹרֶת שֶׁשֱּׁזָפַתְנִי הַשָּׁמֶשׁ בְּנֵי אִמִּי נִחֲרוּ־בִי שָׂמֻנִי נֹטֵרָה אֶת־הַכְּרָמִים כַּרְמִי שֶׁלִּי לֹא נָטָרְתִּי׃
englishText: Look not upon me, that I am swarthy, That the sun hath tanned me; My mother’s sons were incensed against me, They made me keeper of the vineyards; But mine own vineyard have I not kept.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הַגִּידָה לִּי שֶׁאָהֲבָה נַפְשִׁי אֵיכָה תִרְעֶה אֵיכָה תַּרְבִּיץ בַּצָּהֳרָיִם שַׁלָּמָה אֶהְיֶה כְּעֹטְיָה עַל עֶדְרֵי חֲבֵרֶיךָ׃
englishText: Tell me, O thou whom my soul loveth, Where thou feedest, where thou makest thy flock to rest at noon; For why should I be as one that veileth herself Beside the flocks of thy companions?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אִם־לֹא תֵדְעִי לָךְ הַיָּפָה בַּנָּשִׁים צְאִי־לָךְ בְּעִקְבֵי הַצֹּאן וּרְעִי אֶת־גְּדִיֹּתַיִךְ עַל מִשְׁכְּנוֹת הָרֹעִים׃
englishText: If thou know not, O thou fairest among women, Go thy way forth by the footsteps of the flock And feed thy kids, beside the shepherds’tents.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לְסֻסָתִי בְּרִכְבֵי פַרְעֹה דִּמִּיתִיךְ רַעְיָתִי׃
englishText: I have compared thee, O my love, To a steed in Pharaoh’s chariots.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: נָאווּ לְחָיַיִךְ בַּתֹּרִים צַוָּארֵךְ בַּחֲרוּזִים׃
englishText: Thy cheeks are comely with circlets, Thy neck with beads.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: תּוֹרֵי זָהָב נַעֲשֶׂה־לָּךְ עִם נְקֻדּוֹת הַכָּסֶף׃
englishText: We will make thee circlets of gold With studs of silver.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עַד־שֶׁהַמֶּלֶךְ בִּמְסִבּוֹ נִרְדִּי נָתַן רֵיחוֹ׃
englishText: While the king sat at his table, My spikenard sent forth its fragrance.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: צְרוֹר הַמֹּר דּוֹדִי לִי בֵּין שָׁדַי יָלִין׃
englishText: My beloved is unto me as a bag of myrrh, That lieth betwixt my breasts.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֶשְׁכֹּל הַכֹּפֶר דּוֹדִי לִי בְּכַרְמֵי עֵין גֶּדִי׃
englishText: My beloved is unto me as a cluster of henna In the vineyards of En-gedi.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִנָּךְ יָפָה רַעְיָתִי הִנָּךְ יָפָה עֵינַיִךְ יוֹנִים׃
englishText: Behold, thou art fair, my love; behold, thou art fair; Thine eyes are as doves. .
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִנְּךָ יָפֶה דוֹדִי אַף נָעִים אַף־עַרְשֵׂנוּ רַעֲנָנָה׃
englishText: Behold, thou art fair, my beloved, yea, pleasant; Also our couch is leafy.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: קֹרוֹת בָּתֵּינוּ אֲרָזִים רחיטנו [רַהִיטֵנוּ] בְּרוֹתִים׃
englishText: The beams of our houses are cedars, And our panels are cypresses.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֲנִי חֲבַצֶּלֶת הַשָּׁרוֹן שׁוֹשַׁנַּת הָעֲמָקִים׃
englishText: I am a rose of Sharon, A lily of the valleys.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כְּשׁוֹשַׁנָּה בֵּין הַחוֹחִים כֵּן רַעְיָתִי בֵּין הַבָּנוֹת׃
englishText: As a lily among thorns, So is my love among the daughters.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כְּתַפּוּחַ בַּעֲצֵי הַיַּעַר כֵּן דּוֹדִי בֵּין הַבָּנִים בְּצִלּוֹ חִמַּדְתִּי וְיָשַׁבְתִּי וּפִרְיוֹ מָתוֹק לְחִכִּי׃
englishText: As an apple-tree among the trees of the wood, So is my beloved among the sons. Under its shadow I delighted to sit, And its fruit was sweet to my taste.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הֱבִיאַנִי אֶל־בֵּית הַיָּיִן וְדִגְלוֹ עָלַי אַהֲבָה׃
englishText: He hath brought me to the banqueting-house, And his banner over me is love.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: סַמְּכוּנִי בָּאֲשִׁישׁוֹת רַפְּדוּנִי בַּתַּפּוּחִים כִּי־חוֹלַת אַהֲבָה אָנִי׃
englishText: ’Stay ye me with dainties, refresh me with apples; For I am love-sick.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שְׂמֹאלוֹ תַּחַת לְרֹאשִׁי וִימִינוֹ תְּחַבְּקֵנִי׃
englishText: Let his left hand be under my head, And his right hand embrace me.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִשְׁבַּעְתִּי אֶתְכֶם בְּנוֹת יְרוּשָׁלִַם בִּצְבָאוֹת אוֹ בְּאַיְלוֹת הַשָּׂדֶה אִם־תָּעִירוּ וְאִם־תְּעוֹרְרוּ אֶת־הָאַהֲבָה עַד שֶׁתֶּחְפָּץ׃
englishText: ’I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, By the gazelles, and by the hinds of the field, That ye awaken not, nor stir up love, until it please.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: קוֹל דּוֹדִי הִנֵּה־זֶה בָּא מְדַלֵּג עַל־הֶהָרִים מְקַפֵּץ עַל־הַגְּבָעוֹת׃
englishText: Hark! my beloved! behold, he cometh, Leaping upon the mountains, skipping upon the hills.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: דּוֹמֶה דוֹדִי לִצְבִי אוֹ לְעֹפֶר הָאַיָּלִים הִנֵּה־זֶה עוֹמֵד אַחַר כָּתְלֵנוּ מַשְׁגִּיחַ מִן־הַחֲלֹּנוֹת מֵצִיץ מִן־הַחֲרַכִּים׃
englishText: My beloved is like a gazelle or a young hart; Behold, he standeth behind our wall, He looketh in through the windows, He peereth through the lattice.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עָנָה דוֹדִי וְאָמַר לִי קוּמִי לָךְ רַעְיָתִי יָפָתִי וּלְכִי־לָךְ׃
englishText: My beloved spoke, and said unto me: ‘Rise up, my love, my fair one, and come away.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כִּי־הִנֵּה הסתו [הַסְּתָיו] עָבָר הַגֶּשֶׁם חָלַף הָלַךְ לוֹ׃
englishText: For, lo, the winter is past, The rain is over and gone;
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הַנִּצָּנִים נִרְאוּ בָאָרֶץ עֵת הַזָּמִיר הִגִּיעַ וְקוֹל הַתּוֹר נִשְׁמַע בְּאַרְצֵנוּ׃
englishText: The flowers appear on the earth; The time of singing is come, And the voice of the turtle is heard in our land;
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הַתְּאֵנָה חָנְטָה פַגֶּיהָ וְהַגְּפָנִים סְמָדַר נָתְנוּ רֵיחַ קוּמִי לכי [לָךְ] רַעְיָתִי יָפָתִי וּלְכִי־לָךְ׃
englishText: The fig-tree putteth forth her green figs, And the vines in blossom give forth their fragrance. Arise, my love, my fair one, and come away.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יוֹנָתִי בְּחַגְוֵי הַסֶּלַע בְּסֵתֶר הַמַּדְרֵגָה הַרְאִינִי אֶתּ־מַרְאַיִךְ הַשְׁמִיעִינִי אֶת־קוֹלֵךְ כִּי־קוֹלֵךְ עָרֵב וּמַרְאֵיךְ נָאוֶה׃
englishText: O my dove, that art in the clefts of the rock, in the covert of the cliff, Let me see thy countenance, let me hear thy voice; For sweet is thy voice, and thy countenance is comely.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֶחֱזוּ־לָנוּ שׁוּעָלִים שׁוּעָלִים קְטַנִּים מְחַבְּלִים כְּרָמִים וּכְרָמֵינוּ סְמָדַר׃
englishText: ’Take us the foxes, the little foxes, that spoil the vineyards; For our vineyards are in blossom.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: דּוֹדִי לִי וַאֲנִי לוֹ הָרֹעֶה בַּשּׁוֹשַׁנִּים׃
englishText: My beloved is mine, and I am his, That feedeth among the lilies.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עַד שֶׁיָּפוּחַ הַיּוֹם וְנָסוּ הַצְּלָלִים סֹב דְּמֵה־לְךָ דוֹדִי לִצְבִי אוֹ לְעֹפֶר הָאַיָּלִים עַל־הָרֵי בָתֶר׃
englishText: Until the day breathe, and the shadows flee away, Turn, my beloved, and be thou like a gazelle or a young hart Upon the mountains of spices.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עַל־מִשְׁכָּבִי בַּלֵּילוֹת בִּקַּשְׁתִּי אֵת שֶׁאָהֲבָה נַפְשִׁי בִּקַּשְׁתִּיו וְלֹא מְצָאתִיו׃
englishText: By night on my bed I sought him whom my soul loveth; I sought him, but I found him not.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אָקוּמָה נָּא וַאֲסוֹבְבָה בָעִיר בַּשְּׁוָקִים וּבָרְחֹבוֹת אֲבַקְשָׁה אֵת שֶׁאָהֲבָה נַפְשִׁי בִּקַּשְׁתִּיו וְלֹא מְצָאתִיו׃
englishText: ’I will rise now, and go about the city, In the streets and in the broad ways, I will seek him whom my soul loveth.’ I sought him, but I found him not.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מְצָאוּנִי הַשֹּׁמְרִים הַסֹּבְבִים בָּעִיר אֵת שֶׁאָהֲבָה נַפְשִׁי רְאִיתֶם׃
englishText: The watchmen that go about the city found me: ‘Saw ye him whom my soul loveth?’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כִּמְעַט שֶׁעָבַרְתִּי מֵהֶם עַד שֶׁמָּצָאתִי אֵת שֶׁאָהֲבָה נַפְשִׁי אֲחַזְתִּיו וְלֹא אַרְפֶּנּוּ עַד־שֶׁהֲבֵיאתִיו אֶל־בֵּית אִמִּי וְאֶל־חֶדֶר הוֹרָתִי׃
englishText: Scarce had I passed from them, When I found him whom my soul loveth: I held him, and would not let him go, Until I had brought him into my mother’s house, And into the chamber of her that conceived me.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִשְׁבַּעְתִּי אֶתְכֶם בְּנוֹת יְרוּשָׁלִַם בִּצְבָאוֹת אוֹ בְּאַיְלוֹת הַשָּׂדֶה אִם־תָּעִירוּ וְאִם־תְּעוֹרְרוּ אֶת־הָאַהֲבָה עַד שֶׁתֶּחְפָּץ׃
englishText: ’I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, By the gazelles, and by the hinds of the field, That ye awaken not, nor stir up love, Until it please.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִי זֹאת עֹלָה מִן־הַמִּדְבָּר כְּתִימֲרוֹת עָשָׁן מְקֻטֶּרֶת מוֹר וּלְבוֹנָה מִכֹּל אַבְקַת רוֹכֵל׃
englishText: Who is this that cometh up out of the wilderness Like pillars of smoke, Perfumed with myrrh and frankincense, With all powders of the merchant?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִנֵּה מִטָּתוֹ שֶׁלִּשְׁלֹמֹה שִׁשִּׁים גִּבֹּרִים סָבִיב לָהּ מִגִּבֹּרֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל׃
englishText: Behold, it is the litter of Solomon; Threescore mighty men are about it, Of the mighty men of Israel.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כֻּלָּם אֲחֻזֵי חֶרֶב מְלֻמְּדֵי מִלְחָמָה אִישׁ חַרְבּוֹ עַל־יְרֵכוֹ מִפַּחַד בַּלֵּילּוֹת׃
englishText: They all handle the sword, And are expert in war; Every man hath his sword upon his thigh, Because of dread in the night.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אַפִּרְיוֹן עָשָׂה לוֹ הַמֶּלֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹה מֵעֲצֵי הַלְּבָנוֹן׃
englishText: King Solomon made himself a palanquin Of the wood of Lebanon.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עַמּוּדָיו עָשָׂה כֶסֶף רְפִידָתוֹ זָהָב מֶרְכָּבוֹ אַרְגָּמָן תּוֹכוֹ רָצוּף אַהֲבָה מִבְּנוֹת יְרוּשָׁלִָם׃
englishText: He made the pillars thereof of silver, The top thereof of gold, The seat of it of purple, The inside thereof being inlaid with love, From the daughters of Jerusalem.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: צְאֶינָה וּרְאֶינָה בְּנוֹת צִיּוֹן בַּמֶּלֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹה בָּעֲטָרָה שֶׁעִטְּרָה־לּוֹ אִמּוֹ בְּיוֹם חֲתֻנָּתוֹ וּבְיוֹם שִׂמְחַת לִבּוֹ׃
englishText: Go forth, O ye daughters of Zion, And gaze upon king Solomon, Even upon the crown wherewith his mother hath crowned him in the day of his espousals, And in the day of the gladness of his heart.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִנָּךְ יָפָה רַעְיָתִי הִנָּךְ יָפָה עֵינַיִךְ יוֹנִים מִבַּעַד לְצַמָּתֵךְ שַׂעְרֵךְ כְּעֵדֶר הָעִזִּים שֶׁגָּלְשׁוּ מֵהַר גִּלְעָד׃
englishText: Behold, thou art fair, my love; behold, thou art fair; Thine eyes are as doves behind thy veil; Thy hair is as a flock of goats, that trail down from mount Gilead.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שִׁנַּיִךְ כְּעֵדֶר הַקְּצוּבוֹת שֶׁעָלוּ מִן־הָרַחְצָה שֶׁכֻּלָּם מַתְאִימוֹת וְשַׁכֻּלָה אֵין בָּהֶם׃
englishText: Thy teeth are like a flock of ewes all shaped alike, Which are come up from the washing; Whereof all are paired, and none faileth among them.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כְּחוּט הַשָּׁנִי שִׂפְתֹתַיִךְ וּמִדְבָּרֵיךְ נָאוֶה כְּפֶלַח הָרִמּוֹן רַקָּתֵךְ מִבַּעַד לְצַמָּתֵךְ׃
englishText: Thy lips are like a thread of scarlet, And thy mouth is comely; Thy temples are like a pomegranate split open Behind thy veil.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כְּמִגְדַּל דָּוִיד צַוָּארֵךְ בָּנוּי לְתַלְפִּיּוֹת אֶלֶף הַמָּגֵן תָּלוּי עָלָיו כֹּל שִׁלְטֵי הַגִּבּוֹרִים׃
englishText: Thy neck is like the tower of David Builded with turrets, Whereon there hang a thousand shields, All the armour of the mighty men.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שְׁנֵי שָׁדַיִךְ כִּשְׁנֵי עֳפָרִים תְּאוֹמֵי צְבִיָּה הָרוֹעִים בַּשּׁוֹשַׁנִּים׃
englishText: Thy two breasts are like two fawns That are twins of a gazelle, Which feed among the lilies.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עַד שֶׁיָּפוּחַ הַיּוֹם וְנָסוּ הַצְּלָלִים אֵלֶךְ לִי אֶל־הַר הַמּוֹר וְאֶל־גִּבְעַת הַלְּבוֹנָה׃
englishText: Until the day breathe, And the shadows flee away, I will get me to the mountain of myrrh, And to the hill of frankincense.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כֻּלָּךְ יָפָה רַעְיָתִי וּמוּם אֵין בָּךְ׃
englishText: Thou art all fair, my love; And there is no spot in thee.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אִתִּי מִלְּבָנוֹן כַּלָּה אִתִּי מִלְּבָנוֹן תָּבוֹאִי תָּשׁוּרִי מֵרֹאשׁ אֲמָנָה מֵרֹאשׁ שְׂנִיר וְחֶרְמוֹן מִמְּעֹנוֹת אֲרָיוֹת מֵהַרְרֵי נְמֵרִים׃
englishText: Come with me from Lebanon, my bride, With me from Lebanon; Look from the top of Amana, From the top of Senir and Hermon, From the lions’dens, From the mountains of the leopards.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לִבַּבְתִּנִי אֲחֹתִי כַלָּה לִבַּבְתִּינִי באחד [בְּאַחַת] מֵעֵינַיִךְ בְּאַחַד עֲנָק מִצַּוְּרֹנָיִךְ׃
englishText: Thou hast ravished my heart, my sister, my bride; Thou hast ravished my heart with one of thine eyes, With one bead of thy necklace.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מַה־יָּפוּ דֹדַיִךְ אֲחֹתִי כַלָּה מַה־טֹּבוּ דֹדַיִךְ מִיַּיִן וְרֵיחַ שְׁמָנַיִךְ מִכָּל־בְּשָׂמִים׃
englishText: How fair is thy love, my sister, my bride! How much better is thy love than wine! And the smell of thine ointments than all manner of spices!
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: נֹפֶת תִּטֹּפְנָה שִׂפְתוֹתַיִךְ כַּלָּה דְּבַשׁ וְחָלָב תַּחַת לְשׁוֹנֵךְ וְרֵיחַ שַׂלְמֹתַיִךְ כְּרֵיחַ לְבָנוֹן׃
englishText: Thy lips, O my bride, drop honey— Honey and milk are under thy tongue; And the smell of thy garments is like the smell of Lebanon.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: גַּן נָעוּל אֲחֹתִי כַלָּה גַּל נָעוּל מַעְיָן חָתוּם׃
englishText: A garden shut up is my sister, my bride; A spring shut up, a fountain sealed.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שְׁלָחַיִךְ פַּרְדֵּס רִמּוֹנִים עִם פְּרִי מְגָדִים כְּפָרִים עִם־נְרָדִים׃
englishText: Thy shoots are a park of pomegranates, With precious fruits; Henna with spikenard plants,
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: נֵרְדְּ וְכַרְכֹּם קָנֶה וְקִנָּמוֹן עִם כָּל־עֲצֵי לְבוֹנָה מֹר וַאֲהָלוֹת עִם כָּל־רָאשֵׁי בְשָׂמִים׃
englishText: Spikenard and saffron, calamus and cinnamon, With all trees of frankincense; Myrrh and aloes, with all the chief spices.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מַעְיַן גַּנִּים בְּאֵר מַיִם חַיִּים וְנֹזְלִים מִן־לְבָנוֹן׃
englishText: Thou art a fountain of gardens, A well of living waters, And flowing streams from Lebanon.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עוּרִי צָפוֹן וּבוֹאִי תֵימָן הָפִיחִי גַנִּי יִזְּלוּ בְשָׂמָיו יָבֹא דוֹדִי לְגַנּוֹ וְיֹאכַל פְּרִי מְגָדָיו׃
englishText: Awake, O north wind; And come, thou south; Blow upon my garden, That the spices thereof may flow out. Let my beloved come into his garden, And eat his precious fruits.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: בָּאתִי לְגַנִּי אֲחֹתִי כַלָּה אָרִיתִי מוֹרִי עִם־בְּשָׂמִי אָכַלְתִּי יַעְרִי עִם־דִּבְשִׁי שָׁתִיתִי יֵינִי עִם־חֲלָבִי אִכְלוּ רֵעִים שְׁתוּ וְשִׁכְרוּ דּוֹדִים׃
englishText: I am come into my garden, my sister, my bride; I have gathered my myrrh with my spice; I have eaten my honeycomb with my honey; I have drunk my wine with my milk. Eat, O friends; Drink, yea, drink abundantly, O beloved.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֲנִי יְשֵׁנָה וְלִבִּי עֵר קוֹל דּוֹדִי דוֹפֵק פִּתְחִי־לִי אֲחֹתִי רַעְיָתִי יוֹנָתִי תַמָּתִי שֶׁרֹּאשִׁי נִמְלָא־טָל קְוֻּצּוֹתַי רְסִיסֵי לָיְלָה׃
englishText: I sleep, but my heart waketh; Hark! my beloved knocketh: ‘Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my undefiled; For my head is filled with dew, My locks with the drops of the night.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: פָּשַׁטְתִּי אֶת־כֻּתָּנְתִּי אֵיכָכָה אֶלְבָּשֶׁנָּה רָחַצְתִּי אֶת־רַגְלַי אֵיכָכָה אֲטַנְּפֵם׃
englishText: I have put off my coat; How shall I put it on? I have washed my feet; How shall I defile them?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: דּוֹדִי שָׁלַח יָדוֹ מִן־הַחֹר וּמֵעַי הָמוּ עָלָיו׃
englishText: My beloved put in his hand by the hole of the door, And my heart was moved for him.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: קַמְתִּי אֲנִי לִפְתֹּחַ לְדוֹדִי וְיָדַי נָטְפוּ־מוֹר וְאֶצְבְּעֹתַי מוֹר עֹבֵר עַל כַּפּוֹת הַמַּנְעוּל׃
englishText: I rose up to open to my beloved; And my hands dropped with myrrh, And my fingers with flowing myrrh, Upon the handles of the bar.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: פָּתַחְתִּי אֲנִי לְדוֹדִי וְדוֹדִי חָמַק עָבָר נַפְשִׁי יָצְאָה בְדַבְּרוֹ בִּקַּשְׁתִּיהוּ וְלֹא מְצָאתִיהוּ קְרָאתִיו וְלֹא עָנָנִי׃
englishText: I opened to my beloved; But my beloved had turned away, and was gone. My soul failed me when he spoke. I sought him, but I could not find him; I called him, but he gave me no answer.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מְצָאֻנִי הַשֹּׁמְרִים הַסֹּבְבִים בָּעִיר הִכּוּנִי פְצָעוּנִי נָשְׂאוּ אֶת־רְדִידִי מֵעָלַי שֹׁמְרֵי הַחֹמוֹת׃
englishText: The watchmen that go about the city found me, They smote me, they wounded me; The keepers of the walls took away my mantle from me.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִשְׁבַּעְתִּי אֶתְכֶם בְּנוֹת יְרוּשָׁלִָם אִם־תִּמְצְאוּ אֶת־דּוֹדִי מַה־תַּגִּידוּ לוֹ שֶׁחוֹלַת אַהֲבָה אָנִי׃
englishText: ’I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, If ye find my beloved, what will ye tell him? That I am love-sick.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מַה־דּוֹדֵךְ מִדּוֹד הַיָּפָה בַּנָּשִׁים מַה־דּוֹדֵךְ מִדּוֹד שֶׁכָּכָה הִשְׁבַּעְתָּנוּ׃
englishText: ’What is thy beloved more than another beloved, O thou fairest among women? What is thy beloved more than another beloved, That thou dost so adjure us?’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: דּוֹדִי צַח וְאָדוֹם דָּגוּל מֵרְבָבָה׃
englishText: ’My beloved is white and ruddy, Pre-eminent above ten thousand.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: רֹאשׁוֹ כֶּתֶם פָּז קְוּצּוֹתָיו תַּלְתַּלִּים שְׁחֹרוֹת כָּעוֹרֵב׃
englishText: His head is as the most fine gold, His locks are curled, And black as a raven.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: עֵינָיו כְּיוֹנִים עַל־אֲפִיקֵי מָיִם רֹחֲצוֹת בֶּחָלָב יֹשְׁבוֹת עַל־מִלֵּאת׃
englishText: His eyes are like doves Beside the water-brooks; Washed with milk, And fitly set.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לְחָיָו כַּעֲרוּגַת הַבֹּשֶׂם מִגְדְּלוֹת מֶרְקָחִים שִׂפְתוֹתָיו שׁוֹשַׁנִּים נֹטְפוֹת מוֹר עֹבֵר׃
englishText: His cheeks are as a bed of spices, As banks of sweet herbs; His lips are as lilies, Dropping with flowing myrrh.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יָדָיו גְּלִילֵי זָהָב מְמֻלָּאִים בַּתַּרְשִׁישׁ מֵעָיו עֶשֶׁת שֵׁן מְעֻלֶּפֶת סַפִּירִים׃
englishText: His hands are as rods of gold Set with beryl; His body is as polished ivory Overlaid with sapphires.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שׁוֹקָיו עַמּוּדֵי שֵׁשׁ מְיֻסָּדִים עַל־אַדְנֵי־פָז מַרְאֵהוּ כַּלְּבָנוֹן בָּחוּר כָּאֲרָזִים׃
englishText: His legs are as pillars of marble, Set upon sockets of fine gold; His aspect is like Lebanon, Excellent as the cedars.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: חִכּוֹ מַמְתַקִּים וְכֻלּוֹ מַחֲּמַדִּים זֶה דוֹדִי וְזֶה רֵעִי בְּנוֹת יְרוּשָׁלִָם׃
englishText: His mouth is most sweet; Yea, he is altogether lovely. This is my beloved, and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אָנָה הָלַךְ דּוֹדֵךְ הַיָּפָה בַּנָּשִׁים אָנָה פָּנָה דוֹדֵךְ וּנְבַקְשֶׁנּוּ עִמָּךְ׃
englishText: ’Whither is thy beloved gone, O thou fairest among women? Whither hath thy beloved turned him, That we may seek him with thee?’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: דּוֹדִי יָרַד לְגַנּוֹ לַעֲרוּגוֹת הַבֹּשֶׂם לִרְעוֹת בַּגַּנִּים וְלִלְקֹט שׁוֹשַׁנִּים׃
englishText: ’My beloved is gone down into his garden, To the beds of spices, To feed in the gardens, And to gather lilies.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֲנִי לְדוֹדִי וְדוֹדִי לִי הָרֹעֶה בַּשׁוֹשַׁנִּים׃
englishText: I am my beloved’s, and my beloved is mine, That feedeth among the lilies.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: יָפָה אַתְּ רַעְיָתִי כְּתִרְצָה נָאוָה כִּירוּשָׁלִָם אֲיֻמָּה כַּנִּדְגָּלוֹת׃
englishText: Thou art beautiful, O my love, as Tirzah, Comely as Jerusalem, Terrible as an army with banners.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הָסֵבִּי עֵינַיִךְ מִנֶּגְדִּי שֶׁהֵם הִרְהִיבֻנִי שַׂעְרֵךְ כְּעֵדֶר הָעִזִּים שֶׁגָּלְשׁוּ מִן־הַגִּלְעָד׃
englishText: Turn away thine eyes from me, For they have overcome me. Thy hair is as a flock of goats, That trail down from Gilead.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שִׁנַּיִךְ כְּעֵדֶר הָרְחֵלִים שֶׁעָלוּ מִן־הָרַחְצָה שֶׁכֻּלָּם מַתְאִימוֹת וְשַׁכֻּלָה אֵין בָּהֶם׃
englishText: Thy teeth are like a flock of ewes, Which are come up from the washing; Whereof all are paired, And none faileth among them.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כְּפֶלַח הָרִמּוֹן רַקָּתֵךְ מִבַּעַד לְצַמָּתֵךְ׃
englishText: Thy temples are like a pomegranate split open Behind thy veil.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שִׁשִּׁים הֵמָּה מְּלָכוֹת וּשְׁמֹנִים פִּילַגְשִׁים וַעֲלָמוֹת אֵין מִסְפָּר׃
englishText: There are threescore queens, And fourscore concubines, And maidens without number.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אַחַת הִיא יוֹנָתִי תַמָּתִי אַחַת הִיא לְאִמָּהּ בָּרָה הִיא לְיוֹלַדְתָּהּ רָאוּהָ בָנוֹת וַיְאַשְּׁרוּהָ מְלָכוֹת וּפִילַגְשִׁים וַיְהַלְלוּהָ׃
englishText: My dove, my undefiled, is but one; She is the only one of her mother; She is the choice one of her that bore her. The daughters saw her, and called her happy; Yea, the queens and the concubines, and they praised her.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִי־זֹאת הַנִּשְׁקָפָה כְּמוֹ־שָׁחַר יָפָה כַלְּבָנָה בָּרָה כַּחַמָּה אֲיֻמָּה כַּנִּדְגָּלוֹת׃
englishText: Who is she that looketh forth as the dawn, Fair as the moon, Clear as the sun, Terrible as an army with banners?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֶל־גִּנַּת אֱגוֹז יָרַדְתִּי לִרְאוֹת בְּאִבֵּי הַנָּחַל לִרְאוֹת הֲפָרְחָה הַגֶּפֶן הֵנֵצוּ הָרִמֹּנִים׃
englishText: I went down into the garden of nuts, To look at the green plants of the valley, To see whether the vine budded, And the pomegranates were in flower.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לֹא יָדַעְתִּי נַפְשִׁי שָׂמַתְנִי מַרְכְּבוֹת עַמִּי־נָדִיב׃
englishText: Before I was aware, my soul set me Upon the chariots of my princely people.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שׁוּבִי שׁוּבִי הַשּׁוּלַמִּית שׁוּבִי שׁוּבִי וְנֶחֱזֶה־בָּךְ מַה־תֶּחֱזוּ בַּשּׁוּלַמִּית כִּמְחֹלַת הַמַּחֲנָיִם׃
englishText: Return, return, O Shulammite; Return, return, that we may look upon thee. What will ye see in the Shulammite? As it were a dance of two companies.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מַה־יָּפוּ פְעָמַיִךְ בַּנְּעָלִים בַּת־נָדִיב חַמּוּקֵי יְרֵכַיִךְ כְּמוֹ חֲלָאִים מַעֲשֵׂה יְדֵי אָמָּן׃
englishText: How beautiful are thy steps in sandals, O prince’s daughter! The roundings of thy thighs are like the links of a chain, The work of the hands of a skilled workman.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שָׁרְרֵךְ אַגַּן הַסַּהַר אַל־יֶחְסַר הַמָּזֶג בִּטְנֵךְ עֲרֵמַת חִטִּים סוּגָה בַּשּׁוֹשַׁנִּים׃
englishText: Thy navel is like a round goblet, wherein no mingled wine is wanting; Thy belly is like a heap of wheat Set about with lilies.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שְׁנֵי שָׁדַיִךְ כִּשְׁנֵי עֳפָרִים תָּאֳמֵי צְבִיָּה׃
englishText: Thy two breasts are like two fawns that are twins of a gazelle.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: צַוָּארֵךְ כְּמִגְדַּל הַשֵּׁן עֵינַיִךְ בְּרֵכוֹת בְּחֶשְׁבּוֹן עַל־שַׁעַר בַּת־רַבִּים אַפֵּךְ כְּמִגְדַּל הַלְּבָנוֹן צוֹפֶה פְּנֵי דַמָּשֶׂק׃
englishText: Thy neck is as a tower of ivory; Thine eyes as the pools in Heshbon, By the gate of Bath-rabbim; Thy nose is like the tower of Lebanon Which looketh toward Damascus.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: רֹאשֵׁךְ עָלַיִךְ כַּכַּרְמֶל וְדַלַּת רֹאשֵׁךְ כָּאַרְגָּמָן מֶלֶךְ אָסוּר בָּרְהָטִים׃
englishText: Thy head upon thee is like Carmel, and the hair of thy head like purple; The king is held captive in the tresses thereof.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מַה־יָּפִית וּמַה־נָּעַמְתְּ אַהֲבָה בַּתַּעֲנוּגִים׃
englishText: How fair and how pleasant art thou, O love, for delights! .
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: זֹאת קוֹמָתֵךְ דָּמְתָה לְתָמָר וְשָׁדַיִךְ לְאַשְׁכֹּלוֹת׃
englishText: This thy stature is like to a palm-tree, and thy breasts to clusters of grapes.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אָמַרְתִּי אֶעֱלֶה בְתָמָר אֹחֲזָה בְּסַנְסִנָּיו וְיִהְיוּ־נָא שָׁדַיִךְ כְּאֶשְׁכְּלוֹת הַגֶּפֶן וְרֵיחַ אַפֵּךְ כַּתַּפּוּחִים׃
englishText: I said: ‘I will climb up into the palm-tree, I will take hold of the branches thereof; and let thy breasts be as clusters of the vine, And the smell of thy countenance like apples;
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: וְחִכֵּךְ כְּיֵין הַטּוֹב הוֹלֵךְ לְדוֹדִי לְמֵישָׁרִים דּוֹבֵב שִׂפְתֵי יְשֵׁנִים׃
englishText: And the roof of thy mouth like the best wine, That glideth down smoothly for my beloved, moving gently the lips of those that are asleep.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֲנִי לְדוֹדִי וְעָלַי תְּשׁוּקָתוֹ׃
englishText: I am my beloved’s, and his desire is toward me.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: לְכָה דוֹדִי נֵצֵא הַשָּׂדֶה נָלִינָה בַּכְּפָרִים׃
englishText: Come, my beloved, let us go forth into the field; let us lodge in the villages.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: נַשְׁכִּימָה לַכְּרָמִים נִרְאֶה אִם פָּרְחָה הַגֶּפֶן פִּתַּח הַסְּמָדַר הֵנֵצוּ הָרִמּוֹנִים שָׁם אֶתֵּן אֶת־דֹּדַי לָךְ׃
englishText: Let us get up early to the vineyards; let us see whether the vine hath budded, whether the vine-blossom be opened, and the pomegranates be in flower; there will I give thee my love.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הַדּוּדָאִים נָתְנוּ־רֵיחַ וְעַל־פְּתָחֵינוּ כָּל־מְגָדִים חֲדָשִׁים גַּם־יְשָׁנִים דּוֹדִי צָפַנְתִּי לָךְ׃
englishText: The mandrakes give forth fragrance, and at our doors are all manner of precious fruits, new and old, which I have laid up for thee, O my beloved.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִי יִתֶּנְךָ כְּאָח לִי יוֹנֵק שְׁדֵי אִמִּי אֶמְצָאֲךָ בַחוּץ אֶשָׁקְךָ גַּם לֹא־יָבוּזוּ לִי׃
englishText: Oh that thou wert as my brother, That sucked the breasts of my mother! When I should find thee without, I would kiss thee; Yea, and none would despise me.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֶנְהָגֲךָ אֲבִיאֲךָ אֶל־בֵּית אִמִּי תְּלַמְּדֵנִי אַשְׁקְךָ מִיַּיִן הָרֶקַח מֵעֲסִיס רִמֹּנִי׃
englishText: I would lead thee, and bring thee into my mother’s house, That thou mightest instruct me; I would cause thee to drink of spiced wine, Of the juice of my pomegranate.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שְׂמֹאלוֹ תַּחַת רֹאשִׁי וִימִינוֹ תְּחַבְּקֵנִי׃
englishText: His left hand should be under my head, And his right hand should embrace me.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הִשְׁבַּעְתִּי אֶתְכֶם בְּנוֹת יְרוּשָׁלִָם מַה־תָּעִירוּ וּמַה־תְּעֹרְרוּ אֶת־הָאַהֲבָה עַד שֶׁתֶּחְפָּץ׃
englishText: ’I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem: Why should ye awaken, or stir up love, Until it please?’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מִי זֹאת עֹלָה מִן־הַמִּדְבָּר מִתְרַפֶּקֶת עַל־דּוֹדָהּ תַּחַת הַתַּפּוּחַ עוֹרַרְתִּיךָ שָׁמָּה חִבְּלַתְךָ אִמֶּךָ שָׁמָּה חִבְּלָה יְלָדַתְךָ׃
englishText: Who is this that cometh up from the wilderness, Leaning upon her beloved? Under the apple-tree I awakened thee; There thy mother was in travail with thee; There was she in travail and brought thee forth.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: שִׂימֵנִי כַחוֹתָם עַל־לִבֶּךָ כַּחוֹתָם עַל־זְרוֹעֶךָ כִּי־עַזָּה כַמָּוֶת אַהֲבָה קָשָׁה כִשְׁאוֹל קִנְאָה רְשָׁפֶיהָ רִשְׁפֵּי אֵשׁ שַׁלְהֶבֶתְיָה׃
englishText: Set me as a seal upon thy heart, As a seal upon thine arm; For love is strong as death, Jealousy is cruel as the grave; The flashes thereof are flashes of fire, A very flame of the LORD.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: מַיִם רַבִּים לֹא יוּכְלוּ לְכַבּוֹת אֶת־הָאַהֲבָה וּנְהָרוֹת לֹא יִשְׁטְפוּהָ אִם־יִתֵּן אִישׁ אֶת־כָּל־הוֹן בֵּיתוֹ בָּאַהֲבָה בּוֹז יָבוּזוּ לוֹ׃
englishText: Many waters cannot quench love, Neither can the floods drown it; If a man would give all the substance of his house for love, He would utterly be contemned.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אָחוֹת לָנוּ קְטַנָּה וְשָׁדַיִם אֵין לָהּ מַה־נַּעֲשֶׂה לַאֲחֹתֵנוּ בַּיּוֹם שֶׁיְּדֻבַּר־בָּהּ׃
englishText: We have a little sister, And she hath no breasts; What shall we do for our sister In the day when she shall be spoken for?
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אִם־חוֹמָה הִיא נִבְנֶה עָלֶיהָ טִירַת כָּסֶף וְאִם־דֶּלֶת הִיא נָצוּר עָלֶיהָ לוּחַ אָרֶז׃
englishText: If she be a wall, We will build upon her a turret of silver; And if she be a door, We will enclose her with boards of cedar.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: אֲנִי חוֹמָה וְשָׁדַי כַּמִּגְדָּלוֹת אָז הָיִיתִי בְעֵינָיו כְּמוֹצְאֵת שָׁלוֹם׃
englishText: I am a wall, And my breasts like the towers thereof; Then was I in his eyes As one that found peace.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כֶּרֶם הָיָה לִשְׁלֹמֹה בְּבַעַל הָמוֹן נָתַן אֶת־הַכֶּרֶם לַנֹּטְרִים אִישׁ יָבִא בְּפִרְיוֹ אֶלֶף כָּסֶף׃
englishText: Solomon had a vineyard at Baal-hamon; He gave over the vineyard unto keepers; Every one for the fruit thereof Brought in a thousand pieces of silver.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: כָּרְמִי שֶׁלִּי לְפָנָי הָאֶלֶף לְךָ שְׁלֹמֹה וּמָאתַיִם לְנֹטְרִים אֶת־פִּרְיוֹ׃
englishText: My vineyard, which is mine, is before me; Thou, O Solomon, shalt have the thousand, And those that keep the fruit thereof two hundred.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: הַיוֹשֶׁבֶת בַּגַּנִּים חֲבֵרִים מַקְשִׁיבִים לְקוֹלֵךְ הַשְׁמִיעִינִי׃
englishText: Thou that dwellest in the gardens, The companions hearken for thy voice: ‘Cause me to hear it.’
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)

originalText: בְּרַח דּוֹדִי וּדְמֵה־לְךָ לִצְבִי אוֹ לְעֹפֶר הָאַיָּלִים עַל הָרֵי בְשָׂמִים׃
englishText: Make haste, my beloved, And be thou like to a gazelle or to a young hart Upon the mountains of spices.
versionTitle: The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)
